blocks:
Ayahs

الٓمٓ (۱)
Alif, Lam, Meem. (1)
غُلِبَتِ ٱلرُّومُ (۲)
The Byzantines has been vanquished (2)
فِىٓ أَدْنَى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ غَلَبِهِمْ سَيَغْلِبُونَ (۳)
in a nearby land, but they, after their defeat, will be victorious (3)
فِى بِضْعِ سِنِينَ ۗ لِلَّهِ ٱلْأَمْرُ مِن قَبْلُ وَمِنۢ بَعْدُ ۚ وَيَوْمَئِذٍ يَفْرَحُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ (٤)
in a few years. All command belongs to Allah, before this and hereafter, and on that day the faithful will rejoice (4)
بِنَصْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ يَنصُرُ مَن يَشَآءُ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (٥)
at Allah’s help. He helps whomever He wishes, and He is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (5)

Key Points

The word biḍ‘ (بِضع) means a piece, just as the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: "Fāṭimah is a biḍ‘ah (piece) of me" (161). Fāṭimah is a part of my body. Sometimes the meaning intended by biḍ‘ (بِضع) is a segment of time (between three and nine years).

Question: What connection does the victory of the Roman army over the Persians have with the Muslims, such that the Quran states: "يَوْمَئِذٍ يَفْرَحُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ" (On that day the believers will rejoice)?

Answer: The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله wrote a letter to the kings of Persia and Rome, inviting both of them to the luminous religion of Islam. Khosrow Parviz, the Shah of Persia, tore up the letter of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, but the Caesar of Rome showed respect for the Prophet's letter. The Muslims wished for the Romans—who respected the Prophet's letter—to be victorious, but they were defeated, and the Muslims were saddened by this event. Allah, by revealing these verses, gave glad tidings to the Muslims that although the Romans were defeated, they would be victorious in the near future, and that victory would be a cause for the joy of the believers (162). Yes, the believing community should not look only inward, but must also react to the bitter and sweet events concerning other nations.

161) Wasā’il, Vol. 20, p. 67. 162) Tafsīr Nūr ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- One of the signs of the miraculous nature (i'jāz) of the Quran is its truthful and realized prophecies. (The Romans were defeated by the Persians, but the Quran prophesied that the Romans would be victorious a few years later, and so it happened.) «غُلِبَتْ - سَيَغْلِبُونَ»

2- Do not despair after a single defeat. «غُلِبَتْ - سَيَغْلِبُونَ»

3- In victories and defeats, the centrality of Monotheism (Tawḥīd) must not be forgotten. «لِلَّهِ الْأَمْرُ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَمِنْ بَعْدُ»

4- We must attribute both the victory and the military means to Him. «بِنَصْرِ اللَّهِ يَنْصُرُ»

5- The reason for Divine help is His Might (‘Izzah) and His Mercy (Raḥmah). «يَنْصُرُ... وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ»

Explanations

Portrait of Sūrah Ar-Rūm

This Sūrah has sixty verses and was revealed in Mecca. Like many Meccan Sūrahs, the main subject of this Sūrah is the Origin (of creation) and the Return (Resurrection/Hereafter), and the discussions between the people of faith and disbelief regarding these topics. This Sūrah begins with the prophecy of the Romans' victory over the Persians in war, and for this reason, it is named Sūrah "Ar-Rūm" (The Romans). A major part of the verses of this Sūrah addresses the mention of Divine blessings in the heavens and the earth, the system of pairing (zawjiyyah) in plants and animals, the creation of man from dust, the bond between man and woman, striving during the day and sleeping at night, the falling of rain and the blowing of wind, and the life of the earth after its death.

Ayah

وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ لَا يُخْلِفُ ٱللَّهُ وَعْدَهُۥ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٦)
[This is] a promise of Allah: Allah does not break His promise, but most people do not know. (6)

Messages

1- Utilize the fulfillment of divine promises to invite people to faith. «وعد اللّه لا یخلف اللّه وعده»

2- The source of breaking promises is either inability, ignorance, or regret, and Allah is purified from all of them. «لا یخلف اللّه وعده»

3- Breaking a promise (of reward) is ugly, but breaking a threat (of punishment) is a type of mercy. «لا یخلف اللّه وعده»

4- Most people are doubtful about Allah fulfilling His promises. «لا یخلف اللّه وعده و لكن اكثر الناس لا یعلمون»

5- The majority is not the criterion; knowledge, faith, and commitment are important. «اكثر الناس لا یعلمون»

Ayah

يَعْلَمُونَ ظَـٰهِرًا مِّنَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ عَنِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمْ غَـٰفِلُونَ (۷)
They know just an outward aspect of the life of the world, but they are oblivious of the Hereafter. (7)

Messages

1- Short-sightedness, superficiality, and ignoring what lies beyond material things constitute a type of ignorance. «لكن اكثر الناس لا یعلمون یعلمون ظاهراً...»

2- Man must be profound and deep-sighted, not superficial and heedless. «یعلمون ظاهراً... و هم عن الآخرة هم غافلون»

3- The world (Dunya) is not bad; heedlessness of the Hereafter (Akhirah) is bad. «عن الآخرة هم غافلون»

4- Knowledge of the superficial aspects of the material world without attention to the Hereafter is subject to criticism. «یعلمون ظاهراً... عن الآخرة هم غافلون»

5- The world has an outer aspect and an inner aspect, and the Hereafter is the inner aspect of the world. «ظاهراً - عن الآخرة»

6- Attention to the superficial aspects of the world causes heedlessness of the Hereafter. «یعلمون ظاهراً... هم عن الآخرة هم غافلون»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرُوا۟ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِم ۗ مَّا خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۗ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِلِقَآئِ رَبِّهِمْ لَكَـٰفِرُونَ (۸)
Have they not reflected in their own souls? Allah did not create the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them except with consummate wisdom and for a specified term. Indeed many people disbelieve in the encounter with their Lord. (8)

Messages

1- Reflection (Tafakkur) is the cure for the disease of heedlessness (Ghaflah). «هم غافلون او لم یتفكرّوا»

2- Reflection is beneficial and constructive when it is far from noise and negative influences, and based on intellect and innate nature (Fitrah). «فى انفسهم»

3- Creation is both purposeful and time-bound. «خلق... بالحقّ و اجلٍ مُسمّىً»

4- The world is nothing more than a limited duration. «اجلٍ مُسمّىً»

5- The result of sound thinking is faith in the truthfulness and purposefulness of existence and the Hereafter, while the result of thoughtlessness is disbelief (Kufr) and the denial of the Resurrection (Ma'ad). «یتفكّروا - لكافرون»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَانُوٓا۟ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً وَأَثَارُوا۟ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَعَمَرُوهَآ أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا عَمَرُوهَا وَجَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ ۖ فَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (۹)
Have they not travelled in the land and seen how was the fate of those who were before them? They were more powerful than them, and they plowed the earth and developed it more than they have developed it. Their apostles brought them manifest proofs. So it was not Allah who wronged them, but it was they who used to wrong themselves. (9)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding the meaning of «أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ» (Have they not traveled through the land?): "The intent is contemplation and journeying through the history of past nations mentioned in the Quran." (163) Regarding the necessity of familiarity with history and contemplating it, Hadrat Ali عليه السلام addressed his son, saying: Although I do not have a long life, I am so familiar with the history of those who came before that it is as if I have lived the entire age of history. (164)

(163) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. (164) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 31.

Messages

  1. Studying history, traveling, and utilizing the experience of others are paths to knowledge, and abandoning them warrants reproach. «أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا»
  2. Awareness of history and its philosophy, and familiarity with the destiny of previous nations, saves man from superficiality. «يَعْلَمُونَ ظَاهِرًا - أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا»
  3. Historical transformations are based on fixed laws, allowing lessons to be drawn from the past for today and tomorrow. «فَيَنظُرُوا»
  4. Journeying and traveling must be a factor of attention, far removed from heedlessness. «فَيَنظُرُوا»
  5. Look at the destiny and outcome of past nations, not at their daily, fleeting manifestations. «كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ ...»
  6. Before Islam, great civilizations and powers appeared and were destroyed. «مِن قَبْلِهِمْ»
  7. If Allah becomes wrathful, no nation, regardless of its power, can withstand Him. «كَانُوا أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً»
  8. Attention to the destruction and annihilation of great powers is a deterrent against arrogance. «كَانُوا أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً»
  9. Felicity and happiness are not found solely in power and development, but rather in faith and following the Prophets. «أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً... وَعَمَرُوهَا أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا عَمَرُوهَا... كَانُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ»
  10. Being captivated by power and wealth is among the obstacles to believing in the Prophets. «عَمَرُوهَا أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا عَمَرُوهَا وَجَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ...»
  11. In propagation and education, we must use clear methods and arguments. «جَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ...»
  12. Allah is Just and Merciful; the primary cause of the destruction of previous nations was themselves. «فَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ وَلَكِن كَانُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ»
  13. Disobeying the commands of the Prophets is oppression against the self (zulm bi-nafs) and leads to punishment. «أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ»

Ayah

ثُمَّ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسَـٰٓـُٔوا۟ ٱلسُّوٓأَىٰٓ أَن كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَكَانُوا۟ بِهَا يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۱٠)
Then the fate of those who committed misdeeds was that they denied the signs of Allah and they used to deride them. (10)

Messages

1- The deniers and the mockers have no benefit from their actions except a terrible punishment. «ثمّ كان عاقبة الّذین اساؤُ السُّوآى - كذّبوا - یستهزؤُن»

2- The downfall of man has stages: first, sin, «اساؤُ السُّوآى » then denial, «كذّبوا» and finally, mockery. «یستهزؤون»

Ayahs

ٱللَّهُ يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۱۱)
Allah originates the creation, then He will bring it back, then you will be brought back to Him. (11)
وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ يُبْلِسُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ (۱۲)
And when the Hour sets in, the guilty will despair. (12)
وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُم مِّن شُرَكَآئِهِمْ شُفَعَـٰٓؤُا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمْ كَـٰفِرِينَ (۱۳)
None of those whom they ascribed as partners [to Allah] will intercede for them, and they will disavow their partners. (13)

Key Points

The word "یُبْلِسُ" (yublisu), derived from "ابلاس" (iblās), means grief and sorrow resulting from intense despair. The word "Iblis" is also derived from this root.

Messages

1- In discussion and argumentation, we should bring forth tangible evidence for rational issues. "یبدؤُا الخلق ثم یعیده" (He originates the creation, then He repeats it). The initial creation is proof of His power to create another.

2- The return to Allah and presence on the Day of Judgment is compulsory. "الیه تُرجعون" (To Him you shall be returned).

3- Do not look at the intoxication of wrongdoers today, for they have a sorrowful day ahead. "یوم... یُبلس المجرمون" (The Day... the criminals shall be plunged into despair).

4- We should not rely on imaginary intercessions. "لم یكن ... شُفعائوا" (There will not be... intercessors).

5- On the Day of Judgment, false loves will turn into bitterness, disbelief, and aversion. "كانوا بشركائهم كافرین" (They will be disbelievers in their partners).

Ayahs

وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَفَرَّقُونَ (۱٤)
The day the Hour sets in, they will be divided on that day [in separate groups]: (14)
فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فَهُمْ فِى رَوْضَةٍ يُحْبَرُونَ (۱٥)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, they shall be in a garden, rejoicing. (15)

Key Points

Rawḍah (رَوضة) refers to a garden that has abundant water and trees. The word yuḥbarūn (یُحبرون) is derived from ḥibr (حِبر), meaning a state of joy whose effects become visible on the face.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the pure will be separated from the impure. Yatafarraqūn (یتفرّقون). (The Day of Judgment is, on the one hand, Yawm al-Jamʿ (یوم الجمع) (165), the Day of Gathering, and on the other hand, Yawm al-Faṣl (یوم الفصل) (166), the Day of Separation.)

2- The Day of Judgment is the day when non-divine bonds and friendships cease. Yatafarraqūn (یتفرّقون).

3- Faith accompanied by good deeds and actions is valuable. Āmanū wa ʿamilū al-ṣāliḥāt (آمنوا و عملوا الصّالحات).

4- Faith and motivation are more important than action. Āmanū wa ʿamilū al-ṣāliḥāt (آمنوا و عملوا الصّالحات).

5- The condition for salvation and entry into Paradise is faith and righteous deeds. Fa-ammā alladhīna āmanū wa ʿamilū al-ṣāliḥāti fa-hum fī rawḍatin yuḥbarūn (فامّا الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصّالحات فهم فى روضة یحبرون). (165) Shūrā, 7. (166) Ṣāffāt, 21.

Ayahs

وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَلِقَآئِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ مُحْضَرُونَ (۱٦)
But as for those who were faithless and denied Our signs and the encounter of the Hereafter, they will be brought to the punishment. (16)
فَسُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ حِينَ تُمْسُونَ وَحِينَ تُصْبِحُونَ (۱۷)
So glorify Allah when you enter evening and when you rise at dawn. (17)
وَلَهُ ٱلْحَمْدُ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَعَشِيًّا وَحِينَ تُظْهِرُونَ (۱۸)
To Him belongs all praise in the heavens and the earth, at nightfall and when you enter noontime. (18)

Key Points

Some believe that these verses refer to the times of the five daily prayers: morning, noon, and evening. It is possible that the phrase "فسبحان اللّه" and the phrase "له الحمد" are commands for tasbih (glorification) and hamd (praise) presented in the form of a statement.

Messages

1- By stating the destiny of the righteous and the wicked, we should encourage others towards goodness and warn them against evil. «فامّا الّذین آمنوا... و امّا الّذین كفروا...»

2- Denying the verses (signs) and the Day of Judgment originates from human disbelief (kufr) and obstinacy. «الّذین كفروا و كذّبوا بآیاتنا و لقاء الآخرة»

3- The destiny of man depends on his own actions. «كفروا و كذّبوا... فاولئك فى العذاب»

4- During the five times of prayer, the Holy Divine Essence has also said Subhan Allah (Glory be to Allah). «فسبحان اللّه ...»

5- Certain times are more suitable for the remembrance (dhikr) of Allah. «حین تمسون و حین تصبحون»

6- The purification (Tanzih) of Allah and declaring Him free from defects precedes His praise. «سبحان اللّه - له الحمد»

7- Glorification (tasbih) and praise (hamd) are valuable in all places (the heavens and the earth) and at all times. «تمسون - تصبحون - السّموات والارض»

Ayah

يُخْرِجُ ٱلْحَىَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَيِّتِ وَيُخْرِجُ ٱلْمَيِّتَ مِنَ ٱلْحَىِّ وَيُحْىِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ تُخْرَجُونَ (۱۹)
He brings forth the living from the dead, and brings forth the dead from the living, and revives the earth after its death. Likewise, you [too] shall be raised [from the dead]. (19)

Key Points

Regarding the emergence of the dead from the living and the living from the dead, many instances and examples have been mentioned in the commentaries, including: the creation of man from sperm (nutfah) and the creation of sperm from man; a believing child from disbelieving parents, and vice versa. All of these are signs of the absolute power of Allah in the universe and His ability to establish the Resurrection (Qiyamah) and gather all beings (Hashr).

Messages

1- Do not doubt the Resurrection, because the work of Allah is to cause death and to resurrect. (By presenting examples, eliminate ambiguities and doubts.) «یحى الارض... كذلك تُخرجون»

Ayah

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنْ خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ إِذَآ أَنتُم بَشَرٌ تَنتَشِرُونَ (۲٠)
Of His signs is that He created you from dust, then, behold, you are humans scattering [all over]! (20)

Key Points

In the Quran, the phrase "و من آیاته" (and among His signs) is used eleven times, seven of which are in this Surah. In the previous Ayah, the emergence of the dead from the living and vice versa was mentioned generally and concisely, and this Ayah is an example of that general concept.

Messages

1- The best way to know Allah is through contemplation of creation. "و من آیاته ..."

2- The divine signs are countless; what is mentioned is but a few of the many. "من آیاته"

3- Among the created beings, man is the most prominent. (The verses concerning the signs of Allah's power begin with man.) "من آیاته ان خلقكم من تراب"

4- Self-knowledge is the prelude to the knowledge of Allah. "من آیاته أن خلقكم من تراب"

5- The origin of existence for all human beings is dust. (Let us set aside worthless boasts.) "من تراب"

6- In dust, there is no sense or movement, but in man, there is, and this is a sign of the power and greatness of the Lord. "خلقكم من تراب... تنتشرون"

Ayah

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنْ خَلَقَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا لِّتَسْكُنُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهَا وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَكُم مَّوَدَّةً وَرَحْمَةً ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (۲۱)
And of His signs is that He created for you mates from your own selves that you may take comfort in them, and He ordained affection and mercy between you. There are indeed signs in that for a people who reflect. (21)

Key Points

| The remembrance of Allah is the source of tranquility for the heart and soul, «بذكر اللّه تطمئن القلوب»(167), and the spouse is the source of tranquility for the body and psyche. «أزواجاً لتسكنوا الیها»(168)

167) Ra'd, 28. 168) Rum, 21.

Messages

1- Creation has a purpose. «خلق لكم»

2- Man and woman are of the same kind. (Contrary to some superstitious and derogatory beliefs that consider women to be inferior beings or of a different kind.) «من أنفسكم»

3- The spouse must be a source of tranquility, not a cause of tension and anxiety. «لتسكنوا الیها»

4- The goal of marriage is not merely the satisfaction of sexual instinct, but rather achieving physical and psychological tranquility. «لتسكنوا الیها»

5- The role of the spouse is to provide tranquility. «لتسكنوا الیها»

6- Affection is a Divine gift that cannot be attained through wealth, status, or beauty. «جَعَلَ» (Mawaddah [affection] and Rahmah [mercy] are Allah's gift to the bride and groom.)

7- Anyone who, through any action, damages the tranquility, affection (Mawaddah), and mercy (Rahmah) of the family, has deviated from the Divine path and is on the path of Satan. «جعل بینكم ...»

8- The relationship between man and woman must be based on affection (Mawaddah) and mercy (Rahmah). «مودّة و رحمة» (Mawaddah and Rahmah are factors for the survival and continuation of tranquility in shared life.)

9- Affection (Mawaddah) and mercy (Rahmah) are both effective together. (Mawaddah without mercy and service leads to coldness, and mercy without Mawaddah also does not last.) «مودّة و رحمة»

10- Only people of contemplation can realize the constructive role of marriage. «لقوم یتفكّرون»

Ayah

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ خَلْقُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَـٰفُ أَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَأَلْوَٰنِكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّلْعَـٰلِمِينَ (۲۲)
Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the difference of your languages and colours. There are indeed signs in that for those who know. (22)

Messages

1- The creation of the heavens and the earth is among the signs of Allah's infinite power. «و من آیاته خلق السّموات و الارض»

2- The difference in races and languages is a path toward knowing Allah. «و من آیاته... اختلاف ألسنتكم و ألوانكم»

3- Every language possesses its own value and originality, and changing it is neither perfection nor necessity. (No one has the right to belittle other races or languages.) «و من آیاته... اختلاف ألسنتكم و ألوانكم»

4- The uniformity of the form and color of all human beings is incompatible with Allah's creativity, innovation, and uniqueness (as the Originator). «و من آیاته اختلاف السنتكم...»

5- The knowledgeable and understanding person attains divine recognition through the difference in colors and languages, whereas the ignorant person uses color and language as a means of humiliation and boasting. «للعالمین»

Ayah

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ مَنَامُكُم بِٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ وَٱبْتِغَآؤُكُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ (۲۳)
And of His signs is your sleep by night and day, and your pursuit of His bounty. There are indeed signs in that for a people who listen. (23)

Messages

1- We should not take Divine blessings lightly; sleep is also a great blessing. «و من آیاته منامكم»

2- Sleep is a prelude to effort (or striving). «منامكم... وابتغاؤكم»

3- Work and effort to earn a livelihood is a commendable matter and a Divine sign. «و من آیاته... ابتغاؤكم»

4- Sleep is primarily for the night, and sleep during the day is for partial rest. «منامكم بالیل والنهار»

5- Divine grace is vast, but our effort to attain it is necessary. «ابتغاؤكم»

6- The blessings we receive are not due to entitlement, but rather are due to His kindness and Divine grace. «من فضله»

Ayah

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ يُرِيكُمُ ٱلْبَرْقَ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَيُحْىِۦ بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَآ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (۲٤)
And of His signs is that He shows you the lightning, arousing fear and hope, and He sends down water from the sky, and with it revives the earth after its death. There are indeed signs in that for people who exercise their reason. (24)

Key Points

In this Ayah and the preceding Ayahs, four expressions are observed: «یتفكّرون» (they reflect), «للعالمین» (for the knowledgeable/worlds), «یسمعون» (they hear), and «یعقلون» (they reason). To realize these four expressions, four natural stages are mentioned: 1. Man initially thinks. «یتفكّرون» 2. Then he understands and becomes knowledgeable. «للعالمین» 3. The one who becomes aware listens carefully to the words and scrutinizes the points. «یسمعون» 4. As a result of deep listening, he reaches reasoning and contemplation. «یعقلون»

Messages

1- Fear and hope, side by side, are constructive. «خوفاً و طمعاً»

2- The lightning in the sky, the rainfall, and the greening of the earth are not accidental; rather, they are based on a precise and calculated program. «البرق... ینزّل... فیُحى»

3- The system of creation is founded upon means and causes. «فیُحیى به»

4- Autumn and spring, the shedding and growth of plants, are among the signs of Allah's power in creation. «فیحیى به الارض بعد موتها»

5- Knowledge of God must be based on knowledge, thought, and reasoning. «لایات لقوم یعقلون»

Ayah

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَن تَقُومَ ٱلسَّمَآءُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ بِأَمْرِهِۦ ۚ ثُمَّ إِذَا دَعَاكُمْ دَعْوَةً مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ إِذَآ أَنتُمْ تَخْرُجُونَ (۲٥)
And of His signs is that the sky and the earth stand by His command, and then, when He calls you forth from the earth, behold, you will come forth. (25)

Key Points

In these verses, both the creation of man from dust, "خلقكم من تراب" (He created you from dust), is considered a sign of Divine power, and so is death and the emergence of man from the grave. "من الارض اذا انتم تخرجون" (from the earth, behold, you shall come forth). In this verse and the five preceding verses, Allah has addressed the people fifteen times and enumerated His blessings, which is one of the methods of propagation (Da'wah).

Messages

1- The stability of the system of creation is neither accidental nor dependent on others; it is by Divine Will. "تقوم ... بامره" (it stands... by His command).

2- The occurrence of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) takes place by the Will and invitation of Allah. "دعاكم دعوةً" (He calls you with a call).

3- The call of the Prophet of God revives the dead, "ثمّ ادعُهنّ یأتینك سعیا" (Then call them; they will come to you running) (169), let alone the Divine call. "دعاكم دعوة من الارض اذا أنتم تخرجون" (He calls you with a call from the earth, behold, you shall come forth).

4- Belief in the Origin (Mabda') is the prerequisite for belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad). He who established the system of existence will also revive you after death. "تقوم السّماء... اذا أنتم تخرجون" (The heaven stands... behold, you shall come forth).

5- The Resurrection is physical (corporeal). "من الارض ... تخرجون" (from the earth... you shall come forth).

6- The occurrence of the Resurrection is instantaneous, not gradual. "اذا أنتم تخرجون" (behold, you shall come forth). (169) Surah Al-Baqarah, 260.

Ayahs

وَلَهُۥ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ كُلٌّ لَّهُۥ قَـٰنِتُونَ (۲٦)
To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient to Him. (26)
وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ وَهُوَ أَهْوَنُ عَلَيْهِ ۚ وَلَهُ ٱلْمَثَلُ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۲۷)
It is He who originates the creation, and then He will bring it back—and that is more simple for Him. His is the loftiest description in the heavens and the earth, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (27)

Key Points

The intent of «من فى السّموات» (those who are in the heavens) is either the angels who are obedient to Allah's command, or other conscious beings that are not yet known to mankind.

Messages

1- In the divine worldview, all of existence consciously and humbly submit and surrender to the Exalted Allah. «كلّ له قانتون»

2- Allah is unique in all things:

In ownership: «له من فى السّموات»

In worship: «كل له قانتون»

In creation: «هوالّذى یبدء الخلق ثمّ یعیده»

In perfections: «وله المثل الاعلى»

3- Attention to the initial creation is the key to understanding the Resurrection (Ma'ad). «یبدء - ثمّ یعیده»

4- Speak according to the people's understanding. (Although no task is harder or easier than another for Allah, the word «اهون» (easier) is still used.)

5- The attributes of Allah are superior to human imagination, pen, and expression. «و له المثل الاعلى»

6- Do not compare anyone or anything with Allah. «و له المثل الاعلى»

7- The requirement of divine wisdom and power is the occurrence of the Resurrection (Ma'ad). «ثمّ یعیده... هوالعزیز الحكیم»

Ayah

ضَرَبَ لَكُم مَّثَلًا مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ ۖ هَل لَّكُم مِّن مَّا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُكُم مِّن شُرَكَآءَ فِى مَا رَزَقْنَـٰكُمْ فَأَنتُمْ فِيهِ سَوَآءٌ تَخَافُونَهُمْ كَخِيفَتِكُمْ أَنفُسَكُمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (۲۸)
He draws for you an example from yourselves: Do you have among your slaves any partners [who may share] in what We have provided you, so that you are equal in its respect, and you revere them as you revere one another? Thus do We elaborate the signs for people who exercise their reason. (28)

Messages

1- Using examples is one of the methods of propagation (Da'wah) and education. «ضرب لكم مثلاً»

2- What we do not approve of for ourselves, we should not approve of for others either. «هل لكم ... من شركاء»

3- Make your conscience your judge and reflect:

You who do not consider similar, yet subordinate, human beings as partners to yourselves, how can you consider that which is dissimilar to Allah as His partner?!

You who cannot tolerate a partner for a single moment, how do you accept a permanent partner for Allah?!

You who do not accept a partner in benefiting from sustenance (rizq), how do you accept a partner in creation for Him?!

You who fear the interference of people similar to yourselves, how do you allow stones and wood to interfere in the work of God?

You who are not the true Owner, yet are unwilling to share, how do you make stones and wood partners with the Creator and the true Owner?

4- Having a partner causes anxiety. «شركاء... تخافونهم»

Ayah

بَلِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَهْوَآءَهُم بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۖ فَمَن يَهْدِى مَنْ أَضَلَّ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَمَا لَهُم مِّن نَّـٰصِرِينَ (۲۹)
Indeed, the wrongdoers follow their own desires without any knowledge. So who will guide those whom Allah has led astray? They will have no helpers. (29)

Messages

1- The polytheists do not reason and they oppress themselves. «لقوم یعقلون بل اتّبع الّذین ظلموا»

2- The polytheist has no scientific proof for his polytheism, and the source of his deviation is inner desires. «اتّبع ... أهواءهم بغیر علم»

3- Polytheism is oppression against oneself. «اتّبع الّذین ظلموا أهواءهم»

4- Human inclinations are diverse. «أهواءهم»

5- Allah misguides those who follow their own desires instead of following knowledge and intellect. «اتّبع ... أهواءهم... أضل اللّه»

6- The characteristics of the polytheist are several things: Oppression: «ظلموا» Irrationality: «بغیر علم» Misguidance: «أضل اللّه» Lack of support: «ما لهم من ناصرین»

7- The oppressive followers of desire will one day remain alienated and alone. «اتّبع... اهواءهم... ما لهم من ناصرین»

Ayah

فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفًا ۚ فِطْرَتَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّتِى فَطَرَ ٱلنَّاسَ عَلَيْهَا ۚ لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِخَلْقِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلدِّينُ ٱلْقَيِّمُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۳٠)
So set your heart as a person of pure faith on this religion, the original nature endowed by Allah according to which He originated mankind (There is no altering Allah’s creation; that is the upright religion, but most people do not know.) (30)

Key Points

In the previous verses, we read that all of existence is humble before Allah, «كلّ له قانتون», and the beginning and end of existence, the highest attribute (مَثَل اَعلى), and honor and wisdom belong to Him, and polytheism is nothing but illusion and oppression. This verse states: So turn your face sincerely toward the religion. Linguistically, Fitrah (فطرت) means creation, splitting the veil of non-existence, and the creation of a being. It is as if Allah created man in such a way that he inclines toward the truth and is averse to falsehood. This is exactly like a mother's affection for her child, which is not a learned matter, but rather innate and instinctive. In narrations, the phrase «فاقم وجهك للّدین» has been interpreted as establishing prayer, turning toward the Qibla, Islam, sincerity, and monotheism.(170)

170) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- The religious leader of the community must first establish his complete adherence to truth before guiding others. «فاقم وجهك للدّین»

2- Man is inherently inclined toward religion and a supporter of truth. «للدین حنیفاً فطرت اللّه التى فطر النّاس علیها»

3- Contrary to the belief of a group who say: man is like an empty vessel that is filled with customs, traditions, and true or false ideas, and that political, economic, and social systems color him, Allah has entrusted the knowledge of truth and the inclination toward truth within him. «فطرت اللّه ...»

4- The meaning of religion being innate does not negate the need for effort in establishing it. «اقم... فطرت اللّه»

5- Deviation is an anomaly; because the inclination toward the true religion is inherent in the nature of every human being. «فطر النّاس علیها»

6- The innate religion is fixed and enduring. «فطرالناس علیها... ذلك الدین القیّم»

7- Creation (Takwīn) and legislation (Tashrī') are in harmony with each other. «فطرالناس علیها... ذلك الدین القیّم»

8- Moving along the path of religion is moving along the path of Fitrah (innate nature). «فاقم.... فطرت اللّه»

9- Man's innate matters may increase or decrease, but they are not completely erased. «لا تبدیل لخلق اللّه»

10- The truth-seeking inclinations inherent within man do not change with the alteration of place and time. «لا تبدیل لخلق اللّه»

11- Any political, economic, social, racial, or geographical inclination that does not have a source within man is not enduring. «ذلك الدین القیّم»

12- Islam is the only enduring religion. «ذلك الدین القیّم»

Ayahs

۞ مُنِيبِينَ إِلَيْهِ وَٱتَّقُوهُ وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۳۱)
—turning to Him in penitence, and be wary of Him, and maintain the prayer, and do not be one of the polytheists (31)
مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ فَرَّقُوا۟ دِينَهُمْ وَكَانُوا۟ شِيَعًا ۖ كُلُّ حِزْبٍۭ بِمَا لَدَيْهِمْ فَرِحُونَ (۳۲)
—those who split up their religion and became sects: each faction boasting about what it possessed. (32)

Key Points

The word Munībīn (منیبین) is derived from Inābah (انابه), meaning repeated and continuous return (to God). In the previous Ayah, He addressed the Prophet personally, saying: «فأقم وجهك» (So set your face), while in this Ayah, He commands all people to perform inābah (repentance/return) and turn back to Allah. «منیبین الیه» (Turning [in repentance] to Him).

Messages

1- Cultivate the innate and internal inclination towards religion through supplication, repentance (inābah), piety (taqwā), and prayer. «منیبین الیه ...»

2- Piety and prayer are aligned with the innate nature (fitrah). «فطرت اللّه... واتّقوه و اقیموا الصلاة»

3- Repentance (tawbah) and turning back (inābah) must be accompanied by abstaining from sin and performing obligatory duties. «منیبین... و اتّقوه و اقیموا الصلاة»

4- In the light of repentance, piety, and prayer, you gain the power to distance yourselves from polytheism (shirk) and division. «منیبین الیه... و لا تكونوا من المشركین»

5- Individual worship is ineffective if it is not accompanied by distancing oneself from religious divisions. «لا تكونوا ... من الّذین فرّقوا»

6- Establishing prayer is the manifestation of the spirit of piety in man. «واتّقوه و اقیموا الصّلاة»

7- Polytheism (shirk) is not merely worshipping the sun, moon, or idols; anyone who causes division in the religion of Allah is a polytheist. «من المشركین من الّذین فرّقوا دینهم»

8- The polytheists are intent on causing religious division among people. «المشركین... الّذین فرّقوا دینهم»

9- Religious conflicts and divisions prevent the flourishing of the innate nature (fitrah) and the spirit of repentance, piety, and prayer. «فرّقوا دینهم»

10- Sometimes partisanship and being content with what we possess distance us from seeking the truth. «كلّ حزب بمالدیهم فرحون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا مَسَّ ٱلنَّاسَ ضُرٌّ دَعَوْا۟ رَبَّهُم مُّنِيبِينَ إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ إِذَآ أَذَاقَهُم مِّنْهُ رَحْمَةً إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم بِرَبِّهِمْ يُشْرِكُونَ (۳۳)
When distress befalls people, they supplicate their Lord, turning to Him in penitence. Then, when He lets them taste His mercy, behold, a part of them ascribe partners to their Lord, (33)

Messages

1- The harms originate from ourselves, but the mercies are from Allah. «مسّ الناس ضُرٌّ - منه رحمةً»

2- A group of people call upon Allah only during times of hardship and calamity, whereas the believer must call upon Allah at all times. «ضرّ دعوا ربّهم منیبین»

3- The sign of the innate nature (Fitrah) of the inclination towards Allah is that whenever hardships and pressures remove the dust of heedlessness, man turns towards Him. «فطرت اللّه... اذا مسّ الناس ضرّ دعوا ربّهم»

4- Man is weak and lacks capacity; he laments over a slight loss and becomes heedless upon tasting mercy. «ضرٌّ دعوا - رحمة ... یشركون»

5- Prosperity is the ground for heedlessness. «اذاقهم منه رحمةً... یشركون»

6- A group of humans are ungrateful. (As soon as they taste a mercy, instead of offering thanks, they commit polytheism.) «اذاقهم منه رحمةً... یشركون»

7- The supplication of the distressed is answered. «ضرّ دَعَوا... اذاقهم منه رحمة»

8- Human life is a mixture of bitterness and sweetness. «ضُرٌّ... رحمة»

Ayahs

لِيَكْفُرُوا۟ بِمَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ ۚ فَتَمَتَّعُوا۟ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ (۳٤)
being ungrateful toward what We have given them. So let them enjoy. Soon they will know! (34)
أَمْ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَـٰنًا فَهُوَ يَتَكَلَّمُ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يُشْرِكُونَ (۳٥)
Have We sent down to them any authority which might assert what they associate with Him? (35)

Messages

1- Polytheism is a step towards disbelief and ingratitude. «یشركون لیكفروا» Returning to polytheism is a form of ingratitude for blessings.

2- One of the Divine programs and traditions is granting respite to the deviants. «فتمتّعوا»

3- In guidance and education, sometimes threat and reproach are necessary. «فتمتّعوا فسوف تعلمون»

He has many lamps yet walks astray, Let him fall and see his own recompense.

4- Alongside the sharpest threats, we must not neglect logic. «فتمتّعوا فسوف تعلمون أم انزلنا علیهم سلطانا»

5- A belief concerning Allah is true and acceptable only if it is confirmed by Him. «أم أنزلنا علیهم سلطانا»

6- Neither polytheism has a proof nor does the polytheist have logic. «أم أنزلنا علیهم سلطانا»

7- Proof and argument must be eloquent and clear. «سلطانا فهو یتكلّم»

8- Attention to other than Allah may occur to anyone, but its persistence is dangerous. «كانوا به یشركون»

Ayah

وَإِذَآ أَذَقْنَا ٱلنَّاسَ رَحْمَةً فَرِحُوا۟ بِهَا ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌۢ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ إِذَا هُمْ يَقْنَطُونَ (۳٦)
When We let people taste [Our] mercy, they boast about it; but should an ill visit them because of what their hands have sent ahead, behold, they become despondent! (36)

Messages

1- Man possesses a personality that is easily influenced and reactive (passive). «فرحوا... یقنطون»

2- Mercy is from Allah, while misfortunes stem from our own actions. «اذقنا النّاس رحمة - سیّئة بما قدّمت ایدیهم»

3- Man, when far from Allah, is hollow and lacks capacity; with a slight blessing, he becomes intoxicated (with joy), and with a little bitterness and sorrow, he reaches a dead end (despairs). (171) «رحمة فرحوا - سیّئة ... یقنطون» Whereas Allah desires from people a patience and capacity such that the loss of blessings does not worry them, nor does the acquisition of blessings make them overly excited. «لكیلاتأسوا على ما فاتكم ولا تفرحوا بما آتاكم» (172)

4- The enjoyment of mercy is certain, but affliction by misfortune is not certain. «اذا... اِن...» (For the enjoyment of mercy, the word «اذا» (idha) is used, which signifies certain occurrence, but for affliction, the word «اِن» (in) is used, which signifies uncertainty.)

171) The tanwīn (nunation) in the words «رحمة» (raḥmatan) and «سیّئة» (sayyi’atin) indicates scarcity and smallness.

172) Hadid, 23.

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۳۷)
Do they not see that Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes, and tightens it? There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith. (37)

Key Points

The word yaqdir (یقدر), derived from qadr (قدر), means both measuring/estimation and restriction/difficulty. However, here, because it appears alongside yabsut (یبسط), the intended meaning is restriction and difficulty.

Messages

1- Paying attention to the fact that sustenance is in the hand of Allah prevents man from despair and hopelessness. «یقنطون اَوَلم یروا»

2- Man must strive to earn a livelihood, but he must know that the decree of livelihood is in the hand of Allah. «اللّه یبسط - یقدر»

3- Throughout the entire Qur'an, the expansion of sustenance is mentioned before its restriction, and this is a sign of His vast mercy. «یبسط - یقدر»

4- We should not attribute the expansion of sustenance to our own cleverness or skill. «ان اللّه یبسط الرّزق»

5- If the expansion and restriction of sustenance are in His hand, why all this greed? «انّ اللّه یبسط الرزق... و یقدر»

6- Only the people of faith take lessons and warnings from observing Allah's provision of sustenance. (But heedless individuals consider everything superficial.) «لآیات لقوم یؤمنون»

Ayah

فَـَٔاتِ ذَا ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ حَقَّهُۥ وَٱلْمِسْكِينَ وَٱبْنَ ٱلسَّبِيلِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (۳۸)
Give the relative his due, and the needy and the traveller [as well]. That is better for those who seek Allah’s pleasure, and it is they who are the felicitous. (38)

Key Points

It is narrated in the traditions (Riwāyāt) that as soon as this Ayah was revealed, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله granted Fadak to Hadrat Zahra علیها السلام. (173) Although the address of the Ayah is directed to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, all people are addressed by it. The intent of «وجه اللّه» (the Face of Allah) is sincere intention in spending (Infaq), just as we read concerning the spending of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام: «انّما نطعمكم لوجه اللّه» (We feed you only for the sake of Allah's Face). (174)

173) Tafsirs Majma‘ al-Bayān and Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 174) Al-Insān, 9.

Messages

1- Allah is the Owner of wealth; therefore, the ways in which wealth is spent must also be specified by Him. «فَآتِ» (So give).

2- In spending and helping, relatives take precedence over others. «ذا القربى... والمسكین ...» (the relative... and the needy...).

3- Relatives have rights and dues upon a person and his wealth. «حقّه» (his due). Yes, kinship is the basis for the emergence of rights.

4- The religious command is in harmony with natural emotions. The instruction to care for poor relatives is the very emotional desire of man. «فآت ذا القربى حقّه...» (So give the relative his due...).

5- The deprived have a right in the wealth of the rich, and one should not remind them of the favor when paying their due. «حقّه» (his due).

6- Islam is the protector of the oppressed (Mustaḍ‘afīn), and the Islamic ruler is responsible for eradicating poverty from the Muslim community. (This is based on the understanding that the Prophet is addressed here as the Islamic ruler.) «فآت... والمسكین...» (So give... and the needy...).

7- Even the wealthy who temporarily face financial hardship must be supported. «وابن السبیل» (and the traveler).

8- Fulfilling the rights of others is valuable, not the accumulation of property and wealth. «ذلك خير» (That is better).

9- Wealth can be a means of drawing closer to Allah. «فآت ذا القربى حقّه... یریدون وجه اللّه» (So give the relative his due... those who desire the Face of Allah).

10- The distinction of Islamic spending (Infaq) over the spending of others is the intention of seeking closeness (Qurbah). «یریدون وجه اللّه» (those who desire the Face of Allah).

11- The command to do good must be accompanied by encouragement. «ذلك خير... هم المفلحون» (That is better... they are the successful).

12- Salvation (Felicity) depends on sincere action. «یریدون وجه اللّه و أولئك هم المفلحون» (those who desire the Face of Allah, and it is they who are the successful).

Ayah

وَمَآ ءَاتَيْتُم مِّن رِّبًا لِّيَرْبُوَا۟ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِ ٱلنَّاسِ فَلَا يَرْبُوا۟ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَمَآ ءَاتَيْتُم مِّن زَكَوٰةٍ تُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ ٱللَّهِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُضْعِفُونَ (۳۹)
What you give in usury in order that it may increase people’s wealth does not increase with Allah. But what you pay as zakat seeking Allah’s pleasure—it is they who will be given a manifold increase. (39)

Messages

1- The amount and quantity of money are not important; the motive and purpose of the payment are important. «من رِباً... من زكاةٍ»

2- The growth resulting from usury (riba) is false and superficial, not real and not with Allah. «فلایربوا عند اللّه»

3- The distinction of Islam is that, in addition to eradicating poverty among the deprived, it also considers the spiritual growth of the payers. «تریدون وجه اللّه»

4- Attention to sincerity (Ikhlas) is a serious matter and must be stated emphatically, because work and effort without a divine motive have no value. «تریدون وجه اللّه» (The phrase «وجه اللّه» is repeated in this Ayah and the previous Ayah.)

5- The sincere payment of Zakat leads to increase and growth. «زكاة تریدون وجه اللّه فاولئك هم المضعفون»

6- The believer must take into account the world and the Hereafter, the individual and society, and the body and the soul simultaneously when discerning profit and loss. (Although, from a materialistic perspective, paying Zakat causes a reduction in wealth, considering the eradication of poverty from society, detachment from the world, and storage for the Hereafter, it is beneficial for the Zakat payer.) «أولئك هم المضعفون»

7- Sincerity elevates the status of man and brings him to a superior point. (Considering the address «تریدون وجه اللّه» [you desire the Face of Allah], it should have said: «أنتم المضعفون» [you are the multipliers], but instead, it says: «أولئك هم المضعفون» [those are the multipliers], as if they have reached a peak point.)

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ رَزَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يُحْيِيكُمْ ۖ هَلْ مِن شُرَكَآئِكُم مَّن يَفْعَلُ مِن ذَٰلِكُم مِّن شَىْءٍ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (٤٠)
It is Allah who created you and then He provided for you, then He makes you die, then He will bring you to life. Is there anyone among your ‘partners’ who does anything of that kind? Immaculate is He and exalted above [having] any partners that they ascribe [to Him]! (40)

Key Points

This verse refers to four Divine attributes, each of which is sufficient on its own for human submission and servitude. The creation of man is an independent proof of the necessity of human servitude. «اعبدوا ربّكم الّذى خلقكم»(175) Allah's provision (giving sustenance) to us is proof of the necessity of our servitude. «فلیعبدوا ربّ هذا البیت . الّذى اطعمهم من جوع»(176) Causing death and giving life are also, each individually, proof of the necessity of man's servitude and submission before the Exalted Lord. «اللّه الّذى خلقكم ثمّ رزقكم ثمّ یمیتكم ثمّ یحییكم»

(175) Baqarah, 21. (176) Quraysh, 3 - 4.

Messages

1- Death and life, past, present, and future, and our sustenance and provision are in the hand of Allah. «خلقكم، رزقكم، یمیتكم، یحییكم»

2- Past actions are proof of Allah's power over future actions. (Creating, providing sustenance, and causing death are signs of Allah's power to resurrect man after death.) «خلقكم - رزقكم - یمیتكم - یحییكم»

3- The method of comparison in teaching and propagation is among the best methods. «اللّه خلقكم ثمّ رزقكم ... هل من شركائكم»

4- Awaken consciences through questioning. «هل من شركائكم من یفعل ...»

5- Non-Divine powers have no independence of their own; they are incapable not only of performing great deeds but also of creating the smallest creature. «من شى ء»

6- We must not consider any of the means, causes, preliminaries, or conditions of creation as partners to Allah. «سبحانه و تعالى عما یشركون»

7- Polytheism (Shirk) is an insult to the station of the Lord, and man must consider Him pure and exalted above any kind of partner. «سبحانه و تعالى»

8- Polytheism (Shirk), regardless of the name, symbol, or form it takes, is false and condemned. «عمّا یشركون»

Ayah

ظَهَرَ ٱلْفَسَادُ فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِى ٱلنَّاسِ لِيُذِيقَهُم بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى عَمِلُوا۟ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (٤۱)
Corruption has appeared in land and sea because of the doings of the people’s hands, that He may make them taste something of what they have done, so that they may come back. (41)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "The life of creatures on land and sea depends on rain. If rain does not fall, corruption occurs on land and sea. And when the sins of man increase, rain does not descend."(177) In Ayah 30 of Surah Ash-Shura, we read: «ما أصابكم من مصیبة فبما كسبت أیدیكم و یعفوا عن كثیر» (Whatever misfortune befalls you is due to what your hands have earned), but know that Allah, due to His mercy and grace, overlooks many of your sins. (If He wished to punish you for all your deeds, no one would remain on earth.) Some worldly hardships and bitterness are a fraction of the punishment for human sins.

177) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- Polytheism (Shirk) is the cause of corruption and ruin on earth. «عمّا یشركون - ظهر الفساد»

2- Human actions affect nature. Man's wrongful deeds prevent the benefit of water and soil and cause the emergence of unpleasant phenomena. «ظهر الفساد فى البرّ و البحر بما كسبت أیدى النّاس»

3- Environmental corruption is due to human actions. «ظهر الفساد... بما كسبت...»

4- Not all punishments are deferred until the Day of Judgment; rather, some punishments are realized in this very world. «لیذیقهم بعض الّذى»

5- Where the goal is warning and reform, sometimes a small amount of chastisement is sufficient. «لیذیقهم بعض الّذى عملوا لعلّهم یرجعون»

6- The purpose of making them taste the punishment for some sins is repentance and return (to Allah). «لعلّهم یرجعون» (Lessons must be learned from calamities.)

Ayah

قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّشْرِكِينَ (٤۲)
Say, ‘Travel over the land, and see how was the fate of those who were before [you], most of whom were polytheists.’ (42)

Messages

1- Purposeful travel (tourism) is recommended by Islam. «قل سیروا...»

2- Preserving relics that serve as a lesson for future generations is necessary. «سیروا - فانظروا»

3- History is one of the sources of knowledge. Studying history and benefiting from past events illuminates the path for the future. «فانظروا»

4- The traditions (Sunan) and laws governing history are fixed. (By studying the causes of yesterday's events, one can identify the path for today.) «فانظروا»

5- A society with a deviant majority will be afflicted by Divine wrath, even if some good people are present within it. «أكثرهم مشركین» (Whenever the majority of people in a region become corrupt, Jihad or migration from that place becomes necessary.)

Ayah

فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ ٱلْقَيِّمِ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِىَ يَوْمٌ لَّا مَرَدَّ لَهُۥ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصَّدَّعُونَ (٤۳)
So set your heart on the upright religion, before there comes a day irrevocable from Allah. On that day they shall be split [into various groups]. (43)

Key Points

The word «یصّدّعون» (yassadda'ūn) is derived from «صدع» (ṣad‘), meaning to break or scatter. Here, it refers to the Day when the believing people will be separated from the deviated individuals, and each group will go its own way.

Messages

1- Religion itself must be firm, stable, and logical, and the religious person must be serious and devoted (loving). «فأقم... للدّین القیّم»

2- Attention to religion must be central to one's actions and done wholeheartedly, not marginalized or done reluctantly. «فأقم وجهك للدین»

3- The religion of Islam establishes all individual and social interests. «للدّین القیّم»

4- We must utilize opportunities. «فأقم... قبل ان یأتى»

5- If we pay attention to the dangers of the Day of Judgment, our religiosity will become serious. «فأقم... قبل ان یأتى یوم...»

6- A complete commitment to religion leads to salvation on the Day of Judgment. «فأقم وجهك... قبل ان یأتى یوم...»

Ayahs

مَن كَفَرَ فَعَلَيْهِ كُفْرُهُۥ ۖ وَمَنْ عَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَلِأَنفُسِهِمْ يَمْهَدُونَ (٤٤)
Whoever is faithless shall face the consequences of his faithlessness, and those who act righteously only prepare for their own souls, (44)
لِيَجْزِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ مِن فَضْلِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (٤٥)
so that He may reward those who have faith and do righteous deeds out of His grace. Indeed, He does not like the faithless. (45)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, He said: On the Day of Resurrection, people will be scattered and separated into different groups. This Ayah explains that separation, showing that the people of faith and the people of disbelief will each receive their reward and punishment. The word 'yamhadūn' (یمهدون) comes from 'mahd' (مهد), meaning a place prepared for rest, and here it means preparation or setting the stage (for the future).

Messages

1- Man possesses free will and chooses faith and disbelief himself. «مَن كفر - مَن عمل»

2- Both encouragement (reward) and warning (punishment) are necessary. «فعلیه كفره - فلانفسهم»

3- The disbelief or faith of people has no effect on Allah. «فعلیه كفره - فلانفسهم یمهدون»

Even if all creation were to become disbelievers, No dust would settle upon the skirt of His Majesty.

4- Do not let faith and righteous deeds make you arrogant, because the reception of every blessing is from the Bounty of Allah. «لیجزى ... من فضله»

5- Disbelief alone is a cause of wretchedness, even if one commits no transgression; however, faith alone is not sufficient, but requires righteous deeds. «مَن كَفر - آمنوا و عملوا الصّالحات»

6- On the Day of Resurrection, the Divine Bounty will encompass those who possess faith and righteous deeds, and not others. «لیجزى الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات من فضله »

7- To enter Paradise, faith alone is not sufficient; rather, righteous deeds are also necessary. «آمنوا و عملوا الصّالحات»

Ayah

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَن يُرْسِلَ ٱلرِّيَاحَ مُبَشِّرَٰتٍ وَلِيُذِيقَكُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ وَلِتَجْرِىَ ٱلْفُلْكُ بِأَمْرِهِۦ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (٤٦)
And of His signs is that He sends the winds as bearers of good news and to let you taste of His mercy, and that the ships may sail by His command, and that you may seek of His bounty, and so that you may give [Him] thanks. (46)

Key Points

The word «ریح» (Rīḥ) is used for strong and harmful winds, while the word «ریاح» (Riyāḥ) is used for beneficial winds. It is narrated from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that sometimes when the wind blew, He would say: «اللّهم اجعلها ریاحا و لا تجعله ریحاً» (O Lord! Make this wind beneficial and do not make it harmful.) (178) Some of the benefits of the wind blowing include: transferring clouds and pressing them together, moderating heat and cold, purifying the air and moving healthy air, delivering oxygen to humans and carbon to plants, pollinating and fertilizing plants, moving ships, generating electricity, scattering seeds around, and so on.

(178) Tafasir Kashshaf and Al-Munir.

Messages

1- Nothing happens by chance; even the blowing of the winds is by the Will of the Wise God (Allah). «یُرسل الریاح»

2- What we receive from the blessings of the wind is part of His signs and His mercies. «من آیاته... من رحمته»

3- The movement of the ship on the sea is by the hand of Allah, not the captain! «لتجرى الفلك بامره» It is Allah Who, by establishing the laws of nature, has provided the means for the ship to move upon the water.

4- Striving for lawful sustenance (rizq) is an injunction of the Quran. «لتبتغوا من فضله»

5- Wind is also a blessing that demands gratitude, even though we may not pay attention. «لعلّكم تشكرون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ رُسُلًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِمْ فَجَآءُوهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَٱنتَقَمْنَا مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ ۖ وَكَانَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنَا نَصْرُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٤۷)
Certainly We sent apostles to their people before you, and they brought them manifest proofs. Then We took vengeance upon those who were guilty, and it was a must for Us to help the faithful. (47)

Key Points

In this Ayah, four divine programs and traditions (Sunan) are presented: A: The tradition of sending the Prophet. B: The tradition of the Prophets having miracles. C: The punishment of the criminals. D: The support (Nusrah) of the believers.

The issue of supporting the believers has been repeatedly mentioned in the Qur'an, including: A: If you help Allah, He will also help you. «اِن تنصروا اللّه ینصركم»(179) B: Those who strive and struggle in Our way, We will surely guide them to Our paths. «والّذین جاهدوا فینا لنهدینّهم سبلنا»(180) C: We will certainly help Our Messengers and those who have believed. «انّا لننصر رسلنا والّذین آمنوا»(181) D: The good end and outcome belong to the pious and the righteous (Muttaqin). «والعاقبة للمتّقین»(182)

The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is the Seal of the Prophets (Khatam al-Anbiya), and there is no prophet after Him. Therefore, the phrase «من قبلك» (from before you) is frequently mentioned in the Qur'an, but the phrase «من بعدك» (from after you) is not mentioned.

179) Muhammad, 7. 180) Ankabut, 69. 181) Ghafir, 51. 182) A'raf, 128.

Messages

1- Familiarity with history is a source of solace. «من قبلك رسلا - فانتقمنا - نصرالمؤمنین»

2- First, cultural work and guidance (Irshad), then dealing with the criminals. «فجاءوهم بالبیّنات فانتقمنا»

3- Opponents of the Prophets are condemned and defeated criminals. «فانتقمنا - اجرموا»

4- Retribution against the criminal is one of the manifestations of supporting the believers. «فانتقمنا من الّذین اجرموا - نصرالمؤمنین»

5- The victory of the believers is Allah's definite promise. (Even if they are martyred outwardly, they achieve their goal.) «كان حقاً علینا»

6- Although man has no claim upon Allah, Allah has made the support of the believers incumbent upon Himself. «حقّاً علینا»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى يُرْسِلُ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ فَتُثِيرُ سَحَابًا فَيَبْسُطُهُۥ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ كَيْفَ يَشَآءُ وَيَجْعَلُهُۥ كِسَفًا فَتَرَى ٱلْوَدْقَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَـٰلِهِۦ ۖ فَإِذَآ أَصَابَ بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦٓ إِذَا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ (٤۸)
It is Allah who sends the winds. Then they generate a cloud, then He spreads it as He wishes in the sky, and forms it into fragments, whereat you see the rain issuing from its midst. Then, when He strikes with it whomever of His servants that He wishes, behold, they rejoice; (48)

Key Points

The word kisaf (كِسَف) is the plural of kisfah (كِسْفَه), meaning a piece, and here it refers to fragments of clouds. Wadq (وَدْق) refers to small particles, and here it refers to raindrops.

Messages

1- Natural transformations are signs of Allah's power, wisdom, and management. «اللّه الّذى یرسل...»

2- The Divine Will is realized through natural means (causes). «یرسل الریاح فتثیر سحاباً»

3- Multiple winds from cold and warm fronts play a role in the formation of clouds. (Al-Riyāḥ (الریاح) is used in the plural form.)

4- One of the benefits of wind is preparing the ground for the descent of rain. «یُرسل الریاح...فترى الودق»

5- The movement of clouds and the falling of rains occur by the Will and desire of Allah. «كیف یشاء»

Ayahs

وَإِن كَانُوا۟ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْهِم مِّن قَبْلِهِۦ لَمُبْلِسِينَ (٤۹)
and indeed they had been despondent earlier, before it was sent down upon them. (49)
فَٱنظُرْ إِلَىٰٓ ءَاثَـٰرِ رَحْمَتِ ٱللَّهِ كَيْفَ يُحْىِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَآ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ لَمُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (٥٠)
So observe the effects of Allah’s mercy: how He revives the earth after its death! Indeed He is the reviver of the dead, and He has power over all things. (50)

Messages

1- Hardships and despair multiply the pleasure of blessings many times over. «یستبشرون - و ان كانوا ... لمبلسین»

Seek little water, bring thirst upon yourself, So that your water may gush forth from high and low.

2- Rain is divine mercy. «فانظر الى آثار رحمت اللّه»

3- Rainfall and the renewed life of the earth are a sign of the establishment of the Resurrection (Qiyamah). «یحى الارض - لمحى الموتى» The scene of death and life is constantly before our eyes, «انّ ذلك لمحى الموتى» and a handful is a sample of the whole heap. «انّ ذلك لمحى الموتى»

Ayahs

وَلَئِنْ أَرْسَلْنَا رِيحًا فَرَأَوْهُ مُصْفَرًّا لَّظَلُّوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ يَكْفُرُونَ (٥۱)
And if We send a wind and they see it turn yellow, they will surely become ungrateful after that. (51)
فَإِنَّكَ لَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَلَا تُسْمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ ٱلدُّعَآءَ إِذَا وَلَّوْا۟ مُدْبِرِينَ (٥۲)
Indeed you cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs [upon you], (52)

Messages

1- Destructive and harmful winds are not unregulated or accidental. «أَرْسَلْنَا رِيحًا»

2- Destructive winds are few compared to beneficial winds. «أَرْسَلْنَا رِيحًا» (While concerning beneficial winds, He said: «يُرْسِلُ الرِّيَاحَ»)

3- Sometimes, a person deviates from the path of Monotheism and enters the path of disbelief and polytheism due to a single bitter incident. «لَظَلُّوا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ يَكْفُرُونَ» (Sometimes a harmful wind transforms the state of joy, «يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ», into a state of failure and ingratitude, «يَكْفُرُونَ».)

4- In guidance and propagation, the perfection of the guide, the method of guidance, and its content are not sufficient; the receptivity of the recipient is also necessary. «إِنَّكَ لَا تُسْمِعُ الْمَوْتَىٰ»

5- A deaf person sometimes understands things by looking at lip movements, but the calamity occurs when the ear is deaf and the person turns his back on the speaker, in which case he does not even understand gestures. This is the example of those who turn their backs on the truth. «وَلَا تُسْمِعُ الصُّمَّ الدُّعَاءَ إِذَا وَلَّوْا مُدْبِرِينَ»

6- Turning away from the truth is dangerous when that turning away is done deliberately and obstinately, and becomes the permanent habit of the individuals. «مُدْبِرِينَ»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَنتَ بِهَـٰدِ ٱلْعُمْىِ عَن ضَلَـٰلَتِهِمْ ۖ إِن تُسْمِعُ إِلَّا مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَهُم مُّسْلِمُونَ (٥۳)
nor can you lead the blind out of their error. You can make only those hear who have faith in Our signs and have submitted. (53)

Messages

1- The duty of the prophets is to guide people, not to compel them to accept guidance. «ما انت بهاد العمى»

2- People's acceptance is limited. «ان تُسمع الاّ مَن یؤمن»

3- Having the spirit of submission (taslim) before the truth is the groundwork for spiritual attainments. «ان تُسمع الاّ... مسلمون»

A salty land does not yield hyacinths, Do not waste the seed of action therein.

4- Faith alone is not sufficient; submission (taslim) is necessary. «من یؤمن بآیاتنا فهم مسلمون»

Ayah

۞ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُم مِّن ضَعْفٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ضَعْفٍ قُوَّةً ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ ضَعْفًا وَشَيْبَةً ۚ يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِيمُ ٱلْقَدِيرُ (٥٤)
It is Allah who created you from [a state of] weakness, then He gave you power after weakness. Then, after power, He ordained weakness and old age: He creates whatever He wishes, and He is the All-knowing, the All-powerful. (54)

Messages

1- Man, at the beginning of creation, is entirely weak. «خلقكم من ضعف»

2- Paying attention to the beginning of birth is a source of remembrance and gratitude. «خلقكم من ضعف...»

3- Do not complain about weakness and old age, for it is the wise plan of Allah. «اللّه ... جعل من بعد قوّة ضعفا»

4- The stages of human weakness and strength have been designed through a wise planning. (Repetition of j'ala [جعل])

5- The beginning and end of man is weakness; let us be grateful during the few days we possess ability and strength. (One strength between two weaknesses) «ضعفٍ - قوّة - ضعفاً» (The weakness of childhood changes into strength, but the second weakness remains, because the word shaybah [شیبة] next to dha'f [ضعف] indicates that this weakness is constant and accompanies old age.)

6- One who is situated between two weaknesses should not become arrogant due to a few days of strength. «ضعف - قوّة - ضعفا»

7- If we are people of insight, the entire existence is a book for study. (Wind, rain, and the life of the earth; the stages of human childhood, youth, and old age.) «من بعد قوّة ضعفا و شیبة»

8- The weakness of the creation does not indicate the weakness of the Creator. «و هوالعلیم القدیر»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ ٱلسَّاعَةُ يُقْسِمُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ مَا لَبِثُوا۟ غَيْرَ سَاعَةٍ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كَانُوا۟ يُؤْفَكُونَ (٥٥)
On the day when the Hour sets in, the guilty will swear that they had remained only for an hour. That is how they were used to lying [in the world]. (55)

Key Points

The word «ساعة» (Sā'ah) refers to a portion of time. If the Day of Resurrection is called «ساعة», it is either due to the speed with which the Resurrection will be established or the speed with which deeds will be judged. (183)

183) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- Whoever became accustomed to false swearing in this world will also employ it on the Day of Resurrection. «یقسم المجرمون» (The criminals will swear).

2- On the Day of Resurrection, the criminals will offer excuses and pretexts, claiming they did not have enough time in the world to believe. «ما لبثوا غیر ساعة» (They did not remain [in the world] except for an hour).

3- The crooked thinking and deviation of the criminals are permanent. «كانوا یؤفكون» (They used to be deluded/turned away).

4- Lying is the cause of all types of crookedness and deviation. «كذلك كانوا یؤفكون» (Thus they used to be deluded/turned away). Meaning: through such lies, they were constantly led astray.

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ وَٱلْإِيمَـٰنَ لَقَدْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِى كِتَـٰبِ ٱللَّهِ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْبَعْثِ ۖ فَهَـٰذَا يَوْمُ ٱلْبَعْثِ وَلَـٰكِنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (٥٦)
But those who were given knowledge and faith will say, ‘Certainly you remained in Allah’s Book until the Day of Resurrection. This is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know.’ (56)

Key Points

This Ayah presents the response of the people of faith against the statement of the criminals in the previous Ayah. They (the criminals) speak of the short duration of their lives in the world or the brief period of Barzakh (the intermediate realm); but the people of faith respond that you have lived according to what Allah determined for the period of the world and Barzakh for individuals, so do not make excuses for your disbelief without reason. Apparently, the reason for the precedence of knowledge ('ilm) over faith (iman) is that knowledge is the foundation and basis of faith.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, there will be conversations between the criminals and the believers. «و قال الّذین ...»

2- Knowledge and faith show their positive effects even on the Day of Judgment. «اُوتوا العلم والایمان» (The knowledgeable believers will also remove doubts on the Day of Judgment.)

3- Knowledge and faith are two Divine gifts bestowed upon individuals. «اُوتوا العلم والایمان»

4- We should consider our knowledge and faith as Divine success (Tawfiq) and not become arrogant. «اُوتوا العلم والایمان»

5- The duration of the period of the world or Barzakh was calculated and written beforehand. «فى كتاب اللّه»

Ayah

فَيَوْمَئِذٍ لَّا يَنفَعُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مَعْذِرَتُهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ (٥۷)
On that day, the excuses of the wrongdoers will not benefit them, nor will they be asked to propitiate [Allah]. (57)

Key Points

Yusta'tabūn (یُستعتبون) is derived from the word ‘utbah (عُتْبه), meaning distress or displeasure. Whenever it is used in the form of isti'tāb (استعتاب), it means repentance and the removal of distress and evil. Although on the Day of Judgment, the criminals will not be permitted to apologize, "و لایؤذن لهم فیعتذرون" (184) (And they will not be permitted to offer excuses), even if they were to apologize hypothetically, their excuse would be of no benefit. "لاینفع الّذین ظلموا معذرتهم" (The excuse of those who committed injustice will not benefit them).

(184) Al-Mursalāt, 36.

Messages

1- The destiny of man and the manner of his presence on the Day of Judgment depend on his own actions. "فیومئذ لاینفع الّذین ظلموا..."

2- Injustice (zulm) is the cause of deprivation from Allah's favor. "فیومئذ لاینفع الّذین ظلموا..."

3- Repentance and apology are effective only in this world and have no utility whatsoever on the Day of Judgment. "فیومئذ لاینفع الّذین ظلموا معذرتهم"

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ضَرَبْنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِى هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ مِن كُلِّ مَثَلٍ ۚ وَلَئِن جِئْتَهُم بِـَٔايَةٍ لَّيَقُولَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا مُبْطِلُونَ (٥۸)
Certainly we have drawn for mankind in this Quran every [kind of] parable. Indeed if you bring them a sign, the faithless will surely say, ‘You are nothing but fabricators!’ (58)

Messages

1- All people are addressed by the Quran. «و لقد ضربنا للنّاس»

2- The Quran is a book of guidance and direction, and parables are among the best methods for conveying truths. Therefore, Allah has presented examples for various contexts. «ضربنا ... من كلّ مثل»

3- The contents of the Quran serve to complete the argument (Hujjah), and whoever understands the truth and has the argument made clear to him, his excuse will no longer be accepted. «فیومئذ لاینفع الّذین ظلموا معذرتهم... و لقد ضربنا للنّاس...»

4- Obstinate disbelievers accept no proof or sign whatsoever. «بِآیةٍ»

5- The goals and statements of the disbelievers and enemies must be anticipated. «لیقولنّ»

6- The obstinacy and stubbornness of the disbelievers should be a lesson for us, prompting us to be more resilient in the path of truth. «ان أنتم الاّ مبطلون»

7- A person who enters the sphere of disbelief and obstinacy sees falsehood as truth and truth as falsehood. «ان أنتم الاّ مبطلون»

8- Because disbelievers lack argumentation, they belittle the truth. «ان أنتم الاّ مبطلون»

9- Disbelievers not only refuse to accept the truth but also attribute falsehood to it. «ان أنتم الاّ مبطلون»

10- Disbelievers consider not only the Prophet of Islam but all Prophets or all believers to be upon falsehood. (Although the one who brought the miracle is solely the Prophet of Islam, the disbelievers, by using the plural form «انتم», seem to consider all Prophets or all believers to be upon falsehood.)

Ayah

كَذَٰلِكَ يَطْبَعُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٥۹)
Thus does Allah seal the hearts of those who do not know. (59)

Key Points

The meaning of "لایعلمون" (they do not know) refers to individuals who do not want to know, just as we read in the previous Ayah: whenever the Prophet brought a sign or a miracle, they stubbornly attributed falsehood (to it). Indeed, individuals who feign ignorance and foolishness are subjected to Divine wrath, not those who are unaware and unbiased.

Messages

1- The punishment for stubbornness is the loss of guidance. «ان أنتم الا مبطلون كذلك یطبع...»

2- Sealing the hearts of the unworthy is a Divine tradition (Sunnah). «كذلك یطبع»

3- Man himself creates the grounds for hard-heartedness within himself. «لایعلمون»

Ayah

فَٱصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَخِفَّنَّكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُوقِنُونَ (٦٠)
So be patient! Allah’s promise is indeed true. And do not let yourself be upset by those who have no conviction. (60)

Key Points

Surah Ar-Rum began with the prediction of victory and concluded with the Divine promise of aid for the group of truth (Haqq).

Messages

1- Religious leaders must be patient. «فاصبر»

2- Faith in the Divine promises is the factor enabling patience. «فاصبر انّ وعد اللّه حقّ»

3- The atmosphere created by faithless individuals should not affect our decision-making. «لایستخفّنّك...»

4- If it were not for Divine grace, warning, and teaching, even the prophets would be vulnerable. «لایستخفّنّك»

5- One of the enemies' tactics is to belittle the leader. «لایستخفّنّك»

6- Those who seek to weaken and humiliate the leader are those who lack certainty in the path of truth (Haqq). «الّذین لایوقنون»

7- Whoever is not patient becomes belittled (or lightened). «فاصبر... لایستخفّنّك»
